targetConcept: Spanish definite articles (el, la, los, las) and noun gender
curriculumSpec: TBD — curriculum-architect is drafting module-007-spec.md (task #1). This validation locks the song selection so the spec + content-builder can anchor to a single primary.
finalDecisionNote: FINAL — primary locked to 'La Vida Es Un Carnaval' (Celia Cruz) per song-validator 2026-05-19. Title leads with 'La', salsa tempo is A1-parseable, and gendered noun pairs (la vida / el cielo / la pena / el carnaval) are dense throughout. NO mid-flight swaps.

primarySong.title: La Vida Es Un Carnaval
primarySong.conceptMatchRationale: Title is 'La Vida' — the article+noun pair the lesson teaches. Celia's declamatory salsa diction makes 'la', 'el', 'los', 'las' audibly distinct. Gendered noun density is high (la vida, el carnaval, las penas, los problemas) — ideal A1 evidence for article-noun matching.
primarySong.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual articles ('el', 'la', 'los', 'las') and 2-3 word article+noun pairs ('la vida', 'el carnaval', 'las penas') as concept evidence. The 4-word title 'La Vida Es Un Carnaval' is safe to display as metadata. NEVER reproduce full lines, chorus, or verses. Quiz items must test the Spanish article-gender concept ('Which article goes with vida?', 'What is the masculine article?'), never lyric recall.
primarySong.verificationNotes: Spotify ID 1BwrMGGhPA6GarWIYaFrW8 confirmed as the canonical 1998 'Mi Vida Es Cantar' track via web search. YouTube ID 7Ho86ggAVrY is the official audio upload (duration ~4:39 = 279s). Both should still get a 1-click human spot-check before shipping.
primarySong.downstreamConflictNote: modules.ts id:7 is currently 'Numbers & Ordinals' (Sofía Reyes — '1, 2, 3'). M7 spec requires this slot to be swapped to 'Articles & Gender' with primary 'La Vida Es Un Carnaval'. Numbers should migrate to another A1 slot — coordinate with curriculum-architect. modules.ts id:9 ('Definite & Indefinite Articles', Shakira — 'La La La') will likely need a different song since Shakira is already heavily used (M6 primary).

pop.title: El Mismo Sol
pop.notes: Modern (2015) upbeat Latin pop — title 'El Mismo Sol' leads with 'el' + masculine noun pairing. Spanish-Cuban-German artist with clean enunciation. Family-friendly, optimistic, no slang. Article density is solid for A1.
pop.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual articles and short article+noun pairs ('el sol', 'la luz'). Title 'El Mismo Sol' is safe metadata.

reggaeton.title: El Perdón
reggaeton.notes: 30-week Hot Latin Songs #1, 300M+ streams, slower reggaeton tempo (A1-accessible). Title 'El Perdón' is a clean masculine article+noun pair. Family-friendly lyrics — about apology/regret, no explicit content. Both artists enunciate clearly.
reggaeton.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual articles + 2-word title 'El Perdón' as metadata. NEVER reproduce chorus.

rnb.title: La Diabla
rnb.notes: Romeo Santos is the 'King of Bachata' — soulful, slow-tempo Latin R&B lane. Title 'La Diabla' is a feminine article+noun pair (with -a ending = perfect canonical example for the gender rule). Romeo's smooth delivery makes articles audible.
rnb.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual articles + 2-word title 'La Diabla' as metadata. Title literally exemplifies the -a → feminine rule. Quiz the rule, not the chorus.

regional-mexican.title: La Llorona
regional-mexican.notes: Iconic Mexican folk ballad. Chavela's slow, declamatory delivery is ideal for A1 listeners parsing every syllable. Title 'La Llorona' is the canonical feminine article+noun pair. Cultural cornerstone — gives the content-builder a strong reading-passage hook for Mexican folklore. Family-friendly (the legend is melancholy but not explicit).
regional-mexican.copyrightSafeNotes: Cite only individual articles + 2-word title 'La Llorona' as metadata. Reading-passage discussion of the folklore must be 100% original prose.

rejectedCandidates.title: La La La (Brazil 2014)
rejectedCandidates.reason: Currently slotted in modules.ts id:9 as the articles song, but Shakira is already M6 primary. Variety rule blocks reuse in M7. Also, 'La La La' is a vocalization, not the definite article — weaker concept evidence than 'La Vida'.

rejectedCandidates.title: El Cuarto de Tula
rejectedCandidates.reason: Strong concept match (el + masculine noun in title) but the tempo is fast and the son cubano improvisation makes individual articles less audible than in Celia's salsa. Save as a B1+ cultural deep-dive candidate.

rejectedCandidates.title: La Bamba
rejectedCandidates.reason: Iconic but the title 'La Bamba' is the only clean article evidence — the rest is mostly repeating the title. Concept density is too thin. Also tempo is fast (~150 BPM).

rejectedCandidates.title: La Camisa Negra
rejectedCandidates.reason: Strong concept match ('la camisa negra' = perfect feminine article+noun+adjective triple) BUT Juanes is already M4 primary ('A Dios Le Pido'). Variety rule blocks reuse.

rejectedCandidates.title: La Bicicleta
rejectedCandidates.reason: Title is a clean feminine pair but Shakira is M6 primary — variety rule blocks.

rejectedCandidates.title: La Gozadera
rejectedCandidates.artist: Gente de Zona ft. Marc Anthony
rejectedCandidates.reason: Strong feminine article+noun title but the song is mostly about listing country names — concept evidence is thin beyond the title. Save for a later geography/cultural module.

rejectedCandidates.title: El Sol No Regresa
rejectedCandidates.reason: Solid concept match but the band name is 'La Quinta Estación' (already provides 'la' evidence in metadata), the song itself has lower article density than Celia's track. Backup-tier.

rejectedCandidates.title: La Vida Es Un Carnaval (En Vivo)
rejectedCandidates.reason: Live version (Spotify 4fKcZxu5Vimh2eoqmJOyno) — picked the studio 1998 version instead for cleaner A1-parseable diction.

copyrightSafetyOverall: All five distinct tracks (La Vida Es Un Carnaval / El Mismo Sol / El Perdón / La Diabla / La Llorona) are mainstream major-label releases with public Spotify + YouTube metadata. Concept evidence is strictly limited to individual articles ('el', 'la', 'los', 'las') and 2-3 word article+noun pairs that exemplify the gender rule. All titles are 2-4 words and safe to display as metadata. Quiz items must test the Spanish article-gender concept, never lyric recall. Reading-comprehension passage on Celia Cruz / Cuban salsa must be 100% original prose.
knownBugFlags: Module 3 quiz dead-end — flagged per role spec, not specific to M7.
'¿Dónde jugarán los niños?' wrong YouTube Music link — flagged per role spec, M7 does not use Maná.
Timer notification issue in YouTube Music — flagged per role spec.

modules.ts id:7 currently is 'Numbers & Ordinals' (Sofía Reyes — '1, 2, 3'). The new M7 curriculum slot per task #1 is 'Articles & Gender'. Numbers content should migrate to another A1 slot — coordinate with curriculum-architect.

modules.ts id:9 ('Definite & Indefinite Articles') currently lists 'La La La (Brazil 2014)' (Shakira) as primary. With M7 now claiming the articles concept AND Shakira already used in M6, id:9 will likely be merged into M7 or repurposed. Track in PENDING.md.

POP: 1-click spot-check 'El Mismo Sol' (Alvaro Soler) Spotify (58WOkUl3O9LvLZjdhiQvIX) and YouTube (aNHwNreDp3A) — confirm 2015 solo version (NOT the Jennifer Lopez collab).
REGGAETON: 1-click spot-check 'El Perdón' (Nicky Jam & Enrique Iglesias) Spotify (7qCAVkHWZkF44OzOUKf8Cr) and YouTube (hXI8RQYC36Q).
RNB: 1-click spot-check 'La Diabla' (Romeo Santos) Spotify (3eCuEXYF3VnbTDHaztfUAv) and YouTube (tYUD9zpSTgw) — confirm Fórmula Vol. 2 studio version, not the live/Tomatito version.
REGIONAL-MEXICAN: 1-click spot-check 'La Llorona' (Chavela Vargas) Spotify (21VxAmhZn3p0kdb0jXu4Ry) and YouTube (J0Ss46HaKNk).
DOWNSTREAM M7/M9 CONFLICT: add to PENDING.md that modules.ts id:7 needs to be repurposed from Numbers to Articles, and id:9 needs a new song (Shakira's 'La La La' is blocked by variety rule).
